$description: Component tokens for Mobile Progress Step — a full multi-step stepper composite that chains 3 Single Step units together. Supports Default (horizontal) and Vertical orientations, Default (number) and icon placeholder modes, and Default text-label or List item container content. The active step position (1, 2, or 3) determines which step is Current; all prior steps are Completed and all subsequent are Next. Figma Reference: Node ID 364-4844

$component.name: Progress Step
$component.description: A complete 3-step stepper that shows the user's position in a multi-step flow. Composed of 3 Single Step sub-components chained left-to-right (Default) or top-to-bottom (Vertical). Each Single Step contains its own connector bar, so the composite has no additional inter-step gap. The 'active step' variant determines which step is Current (blue, active state) — all prior steps are Completed (solid blue dot + check icon) and all subsequent are Next (outlined dot + gray number). Supports icon placeholder mode (where each dot shows a zap/placeholder icon instead of a number) and List item container mode (where label text is replaced by a full ListItem component).
$component.structure: Flex row (Default) or flex column (Vertical) of 3 Single Step sub-components. Each Single Step contains: indicator dot + connector bar + label. The last step's connector bar is hidden (no step follows it). Container is full-width; no additional padding beyond what Single Step provides.

dimensions.vertical-height: 222.5px — height in Vertical orientation (3 steps with 35.5px connectors)

activeStep.1: Step 1 = Current, Step 2 = Next, Step 3 = Next
activeStep.2: Step 1 = Completed, Step 2 = Current, Step 3 = Next
activeStep.3: Step 1 = Completed, Step 2 = Completed, Step 3 = Current

orientation.Default: Horizontal — steps and connector bars arranged left-to-right in a row
orientation.Vertical: Vertical — each step's dot and bar stacked top-to-bottom; label text appears to the right of the dot column

placeholder.Default: Step dots show step numbers (1, 2, 3) for Next state; check-circle for Completed
placeholder.icon: Step dots show a placeholder icon (e.g. zap) for all states; check-circle for Completed

container.Default: Label block shows title + description text lines
container.List item: Label area replaced by a full ListItem component (avatar, name, email, divider)

subComponents.singleStep: single-step.json — each of the 3 step units; see indicator, title, description, and layout tokens
subComponents.progressStepBar: progress-step-bar.json — the connector bars embedded within each Single Step

usage.contexts: Wizard flows, onboarding sequences, multi-step form completion, checkout flows.
usage.features: 3-step stepper, 6 variants (active step × 3, orientation × 2, placeholder × 2, container × 2), horizontal and vertical layout, full HighRise stepper pattern

step-count.description: Mobile progress step total number of steps in the stepper - 3 (the standard step count for this component; each step is a Single Step sub-component)

layout.$comment: Container layout properties for the full stepper composite

direction.description: Mobile progress step flex direction for Default orientation - row (steps arranged left-to-right)

align.description: Mobile progress step alignment for Default orientation - center (step dots and bars vertically centered)

direction.description: Mobile progress step flex direction for Vertical orientation - column (steps stacked top-to-bottom)

align.description: Mobile progress step alignment for Vertical orientation - start (step dots and bars left-aligned)

inter-step-gap.description: Mobile progress step gap between Single Step components - 0px (no additional gap; each Single Step contains its own ProgressStepBar connector that visually fills the space between dots)
